title: Any missing two-way synchronizations in monday.com?
excerpt:
Are you using monday.com and finding that certain tools still don’t communicate as you’d like? At resynced.io, we currently offer a 2-way integration with Google Sheets, and we’ll soon release a Notion integration. 
I’m …

posts.author: Mykola (Mykola Pustovoychenko)
posts.cookedHtml:
Are you using monday.com and finding that certain tools still don’t communicate as you’d like? At resynced.io, we currently offer a 2-way integration with Google Sheets, and we’ll soon release a Notion integration.

I’m interested in hearing which systems or platforms you believe need better 2-way synchronization. If you have a specific integration in mind, feel free to get in touch. I’m happy to explore options that could help streamline your workflow.

posts.author: sachinkarma (sachin kumar karma)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Mykola

, I propose leveraging automation platforms like Make.com and Zapier. These tools allow for real-time data flow between monday.com and platforms such as Google Sheets and Notion, minimizing manual intervention and errors. With customizable workflows, triggers, and actions, we can create tailored solutions that sync data effortlessly across your preferred platforms.

  • Automated triggers for real-time updates
  • Custom data mapping for structured information flow
  • Error handling to prevent sync failures

posts.author: BruceGosk (Bruce Gosk)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey Sachin, make and Zapier can be very time consuming to create and maintain these scenario’s and zaps vs an app you can install inside of monday. Especially if you are dealing with data from multiple boards/workspaces/accounts.

posts.author: Mykola (Mykola Pustovoychenko)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @sachinkarma

Thank you for your suggestion. Make and Zapier are amazing when it comes to automations like “when something happens in one system then do something in another” .

However, this is not a case for 2-way sync. In order to implement a two-way syncing with trigger-based automation, there are plenty of triggers you should handle on both sides (creation, deletion, all kinds of updates). This is where it becomes very complex. Zapier even states explicitly that they don’t support two-way syncing: https://help.zapier.com/hc/en-us/articles/8495908569613-Does-Zapier-support-two-way-syncing

We created resynced.io to cover such use cases.

posts.author: anca.racu (Anca Racu)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m excited to share that Mediaposte Martech has developed a new app designed to enhance your monday.com experience: the Google Sheets Sync Bridge!
This app makes it easier than ever to sync your monday.com data with Google Sheets, allowing seamless two-way data flow between the platforms.

You can explore and start using the app today by visiting
